likes: I like Tally because it is so easy to use and requires no setup. I can create forms in minutes and the integration with CRM systems is straightforward. I also appreciate that no special training is needed to start working with it. The support is good, and I particularly enjoy the data capturing feature, which even captures partially filled data. Using Tally with n8n and Zoho CRM for automation has been seamless. We use Tally.so almost daily for our events, internal workflows etc.
dislikes: There are a few things that can be improved or added. For example, version history would be useful to see who made what changes and to restore forms to the last working version. Also, there should be a polling/voting feature included in a form.
problems_solved: I use Tally for lead capturing and information gathering. It's easy to use, requires no special training, integrates easily with CRM, and even captures partially filled data.

likes: It has everything I need for form creation: templates (not a huge number, but enough, and they’re well made), conditional logic (so you can customize the form based on data you collected in a previous step, for example), and hidden fields to help track the form’s source.
dislikes: I think it would be helpful to add a statistics graph on the form list page so you can quickly see submission traffic. Right now it only shows the total number of submissions for each form, and to view the stats you first have to open the form editor.
problems_solved: You can easily create forms for marketing campaigns on social media, email, or other sources without needing to program anything manually. It’s faster to set up and delivers the same conversion.

likes: Tally is super easy to use and very intuitive. All of the free features met my needs, so I never felt the need to upgrade to a paid plan. It also integrates with our Slack channel, which is a great bonus. This allows us to monitor help request tickets, feedback submissions, onboarding surveys, and any other forms that come through in real time so we can respond quickly.
dislikes: At first, I found their conditional logic a bit confusing. However, it must have been consistent feedback from users, because they recently released an update that addressed the issue and made it much easier to work with.
problems_solved: Tally makes it easy for us to create and manage forms for help requests, onboarding surveys, and general feedback. It’s a straightforward way to collect structured information from partners and customers without having to rely on a complex form builder. The Slack integration is especially useful: submissions land directly in our channel, so the team can keep an eye on requests in real time and respond quickly. Overall, Tally has streamlined how we capture, track, and act on feedback and support inquiries.

likes: I find Tally really easy to use, especially for building forms. It's almost like writing a Word document, which makes it really user-friendly. The forms are not only easy to create but also beautifully minimalistic and clean. Tally allows for easy insertion of various blocks, accommodating different types of data beyond just emails, numbers, and text. Additionally, Tally's integrations with platforms like Airtable and Notion are really helpful, keeping everything synchronized. The setup process was absolutely easy for my team, even for those who aren't tech-savvy, and the pro plan suits a large organization perfectly with its workspace and user management functionalities. It's great that we made the switch from our previous service to Tally.
dislikes: I mean, for example, these things are the dashboards. If I would like to have the information within dashboards, I wouldn't necessarily have to use an integration. Right? So to deploy my dashboard within Airtable with Notion, It's something that's not integrated yet in Tally, but I think that could be improved.
problems_solved: Tally helps us collect data from different users efficiently and build attractive forms easily, even with complex logic. It integrates with tools like Airtable and Notion, synchronizing data and increasing responses. Our team finds it easy to use, even without strong tech skills.

likes: I like that Tally is easy to set up at the very initial stages. Although things got a bit more complex later on, it was easy to start. The support team is really helpful. I emailed them, and they explained everything really clearly, which was much appreciated. Tally was super simple to use, especially when I was comparing different calculators and apps for my calculations. It was the easiest to set up among the options I tested. I like that they have a logic formula with a logic drop-down menu, and the support team helped me understand where to put it and what it means. That was really good.
dislikes: There's a bit of complexity in some of the logic and calculation things, which can be overwhelming from a novice point of view.
problems_solved: Tally helps me with their logic formula and drop-down menu, making it easier to understand where to place them and what they mean.

likes: I appreciate how Tally qualifies our leads and is easy to use. I love the numerous integrations, especially with Meta for conversion tracking, which is crucial for running ads. The new analytics feature is fantastic as it helps optimize our conversion rates. I enjoy the premium feel of the software, where everything works as it should without any bugs or messiness, and the documents and support are reliable. The fantastic support and practical, up-to-date guide and supporting documents made the initial setup very easy.
dislikes: Integration with Calendly - or just better custom embeds on thank you pages in general.
problems_solved: I use Tally for lead qualification. It qualifies our leads and is easy to use. The integrations, especially with Meta for conversion tracking, are crucial. The new analytics feature helps optimize conversion rates. Tally's premium feel means everything works reliably without bugs.

likes: I like that Tally has a Notion-like interface, making it super simple to use. The simple conditional workflows for complex surveys are incredibly helpful, and the pricing is straightforward without lots of features hidden behind successive paywalls. I appreciate the generous free tier and the fact that the base paid tier offers almost everything most businesses would need. The form building process is very easy and elegant without complicating the UI. The great pricing and team features are a plus. Also, setting it up on our own domain with our branding was super easy and quick.
dislikes: A few more native integrations would be nice, but with other zappier like tools you can make anything happen.
problems_solved: I use Tally for collecting guest feedback after trips. It provides simple conditional workflows for complex surveys, easy integrations, and straightforward pricing. We switched from Survey Monkey because Tally offers essential features without expensive paywalls.

likes:
It's super easy to use, but is also very customizable. Conditional logic is something that is not commonly seen on survey platforms that are user-friendly. The way the logic is set up is clear and intuitive. I have used many different survey platforms and Tally is by far my favorite.

There are also several features that aren't relevant to me, but, having the ability to embed video/audio, get signatures, add a CAPTCHA, receive payments, etc. is far beyond what other survey platforms offer, especially as a free version.

I had a positive interaction with customer support when I encountered an error on the survey I was creating. The issue was fixed with minimal effort, but even if it was a larger issue support was willing to work with me on getting it resolved.

The integrations with Google Sheets and Slack are great features.

The guides are very detailed and the templates are plentiful.

I cannot believe the level of access just on the free version, many of the free features are paywalled on other platforms, or don't exist at all. I am using this on a personal level, but I also work with survey platforms at my place of employment and plan on bringing this platform up to my colleagues to see if we can make a use for it as it's better than some of the tools we currently use.
dislikes:
It's hard to say anything I "dislike" about Tally, more like, some small things that could be clearer such as "blocks" being used for both the question field and the answer field. It took me a moment to realize that they're called the same thing but function differently.

If not using text-based input, the area where you add blocks or adjust the settings are quite small, I could see someone with visual impairment having some difficulty finding them.
problems_solved: I needed to create a survey that had ranked choices, which is surprisingly hard to find on a small/non-existent budget. What I found was that and more. Conditional logic has given me much more flexibility on how I could set up the survey to benefit all of those who will take it.

likes: It's ease of use. How professional it looks. It's a really stand out product and the fact of the version I'm using is free is incredibly helpful while I build social groups and my business.
dislikes: I haven't found anything I dislike about Tally.
problems_solved: For now I'm only running a giveaway, another tally I'll create will be an application form which will help filter out individuals who will be a part of a monthly collective. The other is for a social group that allows each person to submit a pre-selected time/day so we can coordinate events around the average selection.

likes:
JARED THE SUPPORT GUY. Im only half joking. Seriously though, ive worked in tech for quite a while, and dealt with a myriad of support systems along the way. Tally is unmatched. I worked with their support team on a (relatively) complex conditional logic system. Their team not only was extremely helpful, but educational as well.

Their team seeks to give you the tools to move forward on your own, when in reality its probably easier for them to just do it for you haha.

Ill be spreading the word in my industry. Give this a try and youll be doing the same. Ill never look backwards to Google Forms ever again.
dislikes:
You should charge money for even this free version, because its so incredibly powerful.

Also Jared probably isnt making enough money. Even if he is, give him more money. Jared is the GOAT
problems_solved: We needed something more powerful with conditional input logic. Nothing out there solved this the way Tally does.

likes: The forms are easy to set up and share. And there are so many free options - it really is the best survey tool I've found in setting up my business. I also love how easy it is to export the survey responses. Many other tools nickle-and-dime you to death with features that are all part of the free Tally experience.
dislikes: The ONLY thing I don't like? That there's no dark option. That's literally the only thing!
problems_solved: There's a lot of experiments that go into starting a business and figuring out if what you're building actually has a market. Surveys are the quickest and easiest way to get information from your target customer. And then actually compiling the results so super easy with the downloadable .csv

likes: I love that Tally is an independent form builder that isn't tied to Google. I like the sleek look, the modularity, and the fact that they seem to really take a balanced view toward adding features that people request. I've used Tally for housing and carpool surveys for our theatre crew, and it's been very easy to integrate into my workflow. I haven't run into any bugs so far!
dislikes: With any modular form builder, there's a learning curve to building questions. I don't use Tally every day, so when I do use it I often need to refresh my memory on how things work and poke around a little bit. It's not too bad, though, and the benefits definitely outweigh the positives.
problems_solved: It's solving "group preference" problems. Theatre crews often travel from away, and they need carpools and housing. It used to be that people just emailed me their preferences and I'd need to organize those into coherent lists myself, or use Google Forms and have a limited scope of things I could do and a sad user interface. With Tally, I can send nice-looking, very flexible forms to everyone and get back an organized list of who needs what. I've saved time and hassle!

likes: I love that Tally is easy to use and that I was able to start for free with the app. Its AI is strong, and everything is easy to find. I found creating forms easier than with Google Form or Survey Monkey, which made my forms look more professional. Even though I was a bit lost at first with the templates, the AI allowed me to achieve the final result in a few minutes.
dislikes: That the interface be available in French would be nice.
problems_solved: Tally allows me to easily manage multiple registrations simultaneously and increase the participation rate in my satisfaction surveys.

likes: I really like Tally because it's so easy to use. The platform has very clear documentation, which helps me understand exactly what I'm doing without any confusion. Additionally, the customer support is great, always ready to assist whenever I encounter any issues. I find using Tally for forms embedded in my company's website is much easier than coding the form directly, saving me a lot of time and hassle. The initial setup of Tally was also very easy, which made integrating it into my workflow seamless. I also appreciate how well Tally integrates with Webflow for connecting to my website and with Make for capturing events, making it a versatile tool in my digital toolkit. I'm so pleased with how it has improved my processes that I would rate my likelihood to recommend Tally to a friend or colleague as a perfect 10.
dislikes: nothing on my mind
problems_solved: I find Tally makes embedding forms in my website easier than coding directly, with clear documentation, easy setup, and great support.

likes: Tally's features are absolutely great - the customization is my fav.
dislikes: Sometimes you just don't have the ability to do everything you want - for example there should be much more built-in styling methods (eg.: the star rating block), mobile scaling isn't always great (for example the upload file block's inner text not auto-wrapping on mobile), etc.
problems_solved: I'm organizing a competition, and Tally is great for registration of project and user forms, it is super easy to use and with good form settings - password protect, auto-mail, etc. Overall it is a must-have tool if you plan on using forms in your organization/business. 100% prefer it over any other form builder!

likes:
The ease of creating a form in the Notion style

Integration with different tools.
dislikes: Sometimes, the need to understand ergonomics to better master the different components and advanced features.
problems_solved: The creation of a complex form to analyze a LinkedIn profile according to a method I have developed.

reviewer.name: Bruno F.
reviewer.role: Influence professionnelle ("expert" LinkedIn) : Conseil & Accompagnement, Formations & Conférences

likes: I am really, really happy with the support that the team has provided, as the instructions were so clear and quick that applying them solved my problem within a few minutes, which I love. The experience was seamless and effective, providing immediate solutions to my issues. Starting with Tally was not difficult, and I found it quite easy to set up, which speaks to its user-friendly design. Tally solves the problem of needing a visually appealing form with different options to engage more effectively with people. The quick processing speed is a standout feature that I find most useful, and integrating Tally with Calendly has been beneficial for booking calls. The user-friendly interface and good support are key factors that would make me consider purchasing Tally again, and I appreciate all these attributes.
dislikes: I find the current customization options limited, particularly in terms of design flexibility. I wish there were more options to have different colors and text styles. Instead of being confined to just one solid color in the background, I'd appreciate the ability to use different pictures as the background for my forms.
problems_solved: I use Tally for creating attractive forms with varied options, enhancing engagement. Its quick support and user-friendly setup solve setup challenges and interaction issues immediately.

likes: It is very easy to start with, have many functions and they don't limit you so you can just start your work... and if it will be too much they will tell you that this load should not be free any more. It can also embed parts of other web content. And with templates, to make simple questionaire is extremely easy.
dislikes: Well, nothing I would not understand why are they doing it... but for example when I wiant to add video to my questionaire I need to upload the video elsewhere. Also When I want to put several images next to each other I need to create the combined image elsewhere first... So for those things, the solution could be better like grid or table layout... and some limitted temporary storage space for video.
problems_solved: Collect internal feedback from distributed team.

reviewer.name: Verified User in Arts and Crafts

likes:
Tally is simple, free and has conditional logic which allows to create questionnaires which are rich but light for the respondent.

I shall non longer use googleforms !
dislikes: it is so rich I need to learn more about Tally
problems_solved:
As a non profit organisation we run on tight budget and we help the city marathon to measure their CO2 impact.

Tally is a perfect solution for us: last year was a heavy google form with little information, this year with Tally we have detailed information on travels through a light questionnaire thanks to the built-in conditional logic and the easy to use bulk dropdown with powerful search.

likes: I love the design, simplicity, and functionality of Tally. I also love the availability, responsiveness, and kindness of the teams. I appreciate when it's easy to use and the intuitive aspect of the platform, which is truly valuable.
dislikes: The initial setup of Tally was not simple. I dedicated a lot of time to it because logic is not my strong suit, but the teams were there to help me.
problems_solved: I use Tally to create a questionnaire as a "lead magnet" and it solved a logic problem in my questionnaire.

likes: Most form builders I've used always look the same and have the most basic functionalities. Google Forms and Microsoft Forms are both fine. But every form that's made in those programs all looks the same, with few options to divert otherwise. With Tally it's so easy to impress other people with my forms because of how clean it is. Not only do I get detailed submission answers, it's easy and accessible for me to integrate with other options.
dislikes: I wish I could use Tally more! I'll never go back to Google Forms after using Tally.
problems_solved: Tally solves my issue of having a clean, easy-to-use form builder. I have great options to guide users through the form as quickly and effortlessly as possible while still gathering plenty of important information.

reviewer.name: Verified User in Public Relations and Communications

likes: I love Tally because it allows me to do everything I could with Typeform, plus more. Their interactive and conditional logic in forms is fantastic. The free tier is incredibly generous, allowing my clients to get started without any upfront cost. Their customer support is amazing, super responsive, and they really listen to their customers. The initial setup was super easy, taking me about an hour to learn how to use it.
dislikes: I would love to see a little more visual design ability.
problems_solved: Tally is more affordable than Typeform, offering similar/better features with interactive and conditional logic, and an intuitive interface. Its generous free tier lets my clients start without initial costs, removing that barrier to entry.

likes: We have tried different solutions for contact forms and Tally has been the best. Feature-rich, generous free tier, thoughtful design. Creating a form feels like creating a Notion document, and it's really easy to embed and connect to storage. We've been using Tally for a long time now and don't see a need to look anywhere else.
dislikes: Haven't found anything to dislike in Tally.
problems_solved: Tally helps us easily create and publish contact and feedback forms for our users.

likes: Very user friendly, easy to use, and highly customizeable. It embeds seamlessly into our event landing pages and it integrates with Google sheets and Canva which is perfect for us.
dislikes: Since the software is highly customizable, there is an extensive list of fonts to choose from. My only wish is that the font list included a preview of each font’s appearance. However, this was not a major issue, as it was still easy to find a suitable font thanks to the wide selection available.
problems_solved: The ability to embed a form into our website smoothly, without any issues or glitches, has been very helpful. We can easily capture all responses from our team.

likes: What I like most about Tally is its ease of use and its easy integration with lots of other applications, notably Airtable for us. The initial setup was extremely easy and free for the vast majority of our R&D.
dislikes: The only thing that we've seen so far is that moving between currency and percentage on fields is a little clunky. And when you copy a form to create another form, all of the integrations are lost.
problems_solved: Tally allows us to easily integrate with Airtable, simplifying our workflow. However, moving between currency and percentage on fields can be clunky, and form integrations are lost when copied.

likes: Ease of Use, Number of Features especially on their free version, and then the paid versions is just like top tier. Google forms, jot form etc. have nothing on this. Def would recommend this out of the I tried creating a form for my business questionnaire. I love tally will be my go to, my recommended, and forever form maker.
dislikes: If there are any please show me, lol like I said I only needed a few options for my forum, sections, to be able to shut part of my questionnaire off after so many submissions and maybe the paid version offers that but even without it i click one question and adjust it and it closes the rest of the form. so i dislike nothing.
problems_solved: Uhm, all of my problems. I love love love love love it.

likes:
Easier to use than other form builders

Great customization and uiux
dislikes:
Need regional based pricing asap

I pay for canva 20 usd per yearr here in Egypt its 120 in the US… 20 usd is manageable per person, maybe 50 for a small business

Personal and small businesses shape most of our 120 million people economy… Majority of them cannot pay 290 usd a year at all… all of egypt uses google forms for that reason

I recommend tally to everyone

But I wish I can send automated emails when people register to my free bootcamp without paying 290 usd per year it’s impossible for me and idk what to do I handle things manually for now but I might need to shift unfortunately

Pls reduce price for egypt so i can change to premium and tell other people about it

I’ll be a tally ambassador haha
problems_solved:
Customizing a form easily

It helps me create registration forms and send things to google sheets

I still have a lot to explore

But my uses are pretty simple so far

likes: I find Tally pretty easy to use, and the free version is robust enough to be useful. I also like that it does what I need in a way that's straightforward and integrates with Zapier, which makes it possible to use Tally in a bunch of different ways, like lead generation and follow up, opening up possibilities. The initial setup of Tally was very easy.
dislikes: The calculation function is not as easy to use as I'd prefer.
problems_solved: I find Tally easy to use, with a free version that's robust enough. The integration with Zapier allows for versatile use in lead generation and follow-up, expanding possibilities.

likes: I love Tally so much! It gives my surveys a way fresher, more interactive, and modern vibe. I also feel like I have way more control over the design and logic compared to G Forms. Plus… let’s be honest, everyone uses G Form, and that look is starting to feel stale and kinda lazy, haha. Tally just stands out. I am taking time to review Tally only because you guys helped me make my surveys stand out, and it is only fair that I return the favor. Keep being awesome!
dislikes: I get that Tally is web-based, but sometimes clicking through everything feels too slow. I really wish there was a way to input lots of data using just text or keyboard commands. As a coder, the mouse just slows me down. Keyboard all the way!
problems_solved: People already hate filling up surveys, at least Tally makes things look fresh and give them that new toy feeling.

likes: The customer service, Jared was incredible and responded quickly and even after multiple emails with questions he solved my issue. All of this from a free account. Thank you!!
dislikes: Nothing, very surprised, i thought i would have to wait days, if at all for a response.
problems_solved: Survey and I am able able to have separate surveys depending on how many questions are answered in the primary survey!

likes: Tally is as simple as it gets for creating forms.
dislikes: I need to be a paying customer if I have to use advance features.
problems_solved: I am using tally to create forms for my beta testing, feedback reviews etc.,

likes: Best form builder ever. Smooth integration with Zapier, very generous free plan, tons of well-thought feature such as redirection to a url with url parameters included which is insanely useful for conversion tracking, also embedding custom fields that are passed to the url of the form is incredibly useful.
dislikes: They might add a "geolocation" input fields
problems_solved: 10 use cases, all different: client onboarding, lead generation with google and meta ads tracking...

likes: I love how easy it is to set up my form and get it published right away. easy to change the form if need to. really simple to make it visually appealing and the best part is the analytics you get. really a great product and i will be using it exclusively. tally is better then similar products that charge $25-30/mo it all just works perfect.
dislikes: nothing at all it all works as you would expect really pleased.
problems_solved: im trying to build an early access list for a app im building. i need name. email and location so i can effectivly roll out my product when i launch, tally has helped me with their analytics to do this.

likes: We are using for all our clients and businesses.
dislikes: Can has built in CRM or lead management system.
problems_solved: Form and lead management.

likes: I have been beyond impressed by the service given by Tally. I have been trying to find compatible and suitable technology for building scorecards for my business and initially bootstrapping a little, I joined Tally thought I'd made a mistake, how wrong I was. I reached out for support and not only did they truly go above and beyond with the support and guidance (which they were quick to deliver) they genuinely care about user experience and usability. There was no generic response, even to the point of sending the links of use to me they highlighted the key versions of interest. For a cost effective and easy to use platform this is the one!
dislikes: There currently is nothing that I dislike.
problems_solved: Assistance in scorecard creation

likes: Its best suited to companies that prefer decentralized structure and are in the early stages. Cant afford a team and are new to ERPs
dislikes: Decentralized, cant upscale and if you are a manager you really cant see whats going on. One user system.
problems_solved: Well, It didnt solve any we had to replace it.

likes:
I chose Tally mainly because it is cost-effective for complex logic and advanced customization. It offers an incredible balance of power and simplicity. Their free plan is also very generous.

However, I opted for the Pro plan to unlock even more features to work on a complex build (my first form). The support team is phenomenal. I encountered a few hiccups along the way, and Laura, Jared, Jose, and Ettore were all invaluable. They were patient and offered detailed workarounds. They also have a list of Tally experts you can hire if you want to hand off the build to someone they trust.
dislikes: While Tally supports advanced logic, it's not visual. With many conditions, it can be challenging to audit or troubleshoot. Resulting in a significant amount of wasted time. A visual logic map or tree view would make complex flows easier to manage. Additionally, it lacks built-in CRM tools and email automation capabilities beyond basic notifications. If you want to nurture leads or tag users, you must integrate with external platforms. Finally, there is no native A/B testing.
problems_solved: I wanted to build a dynamic quiz as a lead magnate. I looked at companies that specialize in quizzes, but even they were limited in what I could do and would also be a hefty monthly bill. For the form part of the build, I needed something flexible that also offered advanced logic at a reasonable price and integrated well with other platforms.

likes: Easy, no fuss forms that can work just about anywhere.
dislikes: I really wish Tally offered integration with Reserve with Google. If I could direct new users straight to a Tally form through a "Book Online" button on my Google Business Profile, I think it would generate more leads. Unfortunately, Tally doesn't support this feature at the moment, which is disappointing.
problems_solved: Submitting forms on any website is straightforward, making it simple to achieve conversions.

likes: Easy to set up a very robust form system.
dislikes: Wish AI was a bit more advanced...but they're working on it.
problems_solved: Form presentation with complex logic

likes:
We’ve been using Tally.so for a while now, and it’s honestly one of the best tools we’ve come across for building forms.

- Effortless to use, even for first-timers

One of the best things about Tally is how easy it is to use. If you’ve ever used Notion, you’ll feel right at home ، you just type a forward slash (/), and a menu of form elements pops up. No need to drag-and-drop or mess with complicated settings. It’s fast, intuitive, and feels like writing a document rather than designing a form.

- Clean and intuitive experience

The user interface is simple and smooth. Even though it offers a lot of functionality, it never feels overwhelming. Everything is well-organized and easy to use, even for non-technical users.

- Customization that makes sense

There are tons of options for building and customizing forms — conditional logic, custom domains, team collaboration, and more but it all feels straightforward. You don’t get lost in menus or settings like you do with other platforms.

- Excellent support for Arabic and RTL

One of the standout features for us is how well Tally handles right-to-left layouts and Arabic fonts. Everything just works out of the box, which is rare to find.

- Great features for teams and scaling

The ability to create multiple workspaces, share forms with teammates, and connect your own domain makes it a great choice if you’re growing or managing multiple projects.

- Better value than anything else we’ve tried

What really surprised us is how affordable it is. It costs less than half of what we were paying for other tools, and those didn’t even support Arabic properly or offer some of the features we now rely on.

If you need a form builder that’s flexible, easy to use, and works great in Arabic — Tally.so is more than worth trying. It’s saved us time, money, and frustration.
dislikes: Nothing, I'm really satisfied with everything they have
problems_solved:
Tally makes it easy for us to collect customer feedback and hear directly from users, without the usual friction or cost that comes with other form tools.

We don’t have to worry about hitting submission limits, overpaying for simple features, or dealing with complicated setups. It gives us everything we need to launch forms quickly, share them easily, and start getting responses right away all while staying within budget.

likes: Tally is the most complete input/form product out there at the free tier, which makes it an excellent choice for anyone looking to graduate from Google Forms. To be honest, I don't think I'll ever go back to Google Forms, and I'll simply always fire up Tally in my future jobs. It's easy to integrate with Google Sheets and Airtable, and it has a ton of features out of the box. Eventually I'll probably pay for a subscription, but for now it's so powerful I'm quite surprised.
dislikes: It's a new-ish product, so they're still releasing features and unfolding the product roadmap. Some key features are surprisingly behind the paywall (e.g. automatic submission confirmation emails), but I guess they need to make money somehow.
problems_solved: Collecting responses as part of personnel recruitment efforts.

reviewer.name: Verified User in Non-Profit Organization Management

likes: You can accomplish a lot without the free plan, and the interface is very intuitive. As someone who loves Notion, I find it especially satisfying that this tool operates with a similar logic.
dislikes: I am unable to integrate directly with systeme.io.
problems_solved: I use Tally to gather applications for my coaching program, which has helped boost my conversion rate. Additionally, I collect reviews through Tally to improve the quality of my services.

likes: I love how flexible and customizable it is. It has far more options than any other form-building site I've used. The interface is sleek too.
dislikes: If you use a lot of conditional logic, it can clog your form and make it hard to see the big picture. It would be nice to be able to collapse some of that when not actively modifying it.
problems_solved: It's helping me engage clients on our website and help them find customized answers quickly. It also gathers information about them for our records.

likes: Tally’s support team is incredibly responsive—far beyond expectations—and the product itself is super easy to use.
dislikes: I wish Tally offered more granular role controls like editor or viewer; having only owner and member feels limiting.
problems_solved: Tally is our company’s main form builder—simple, reliable, and essential for collecting data efficiently.

likes: I loved this! I just joined a new company as Head of People and Talent, I'm still waiting to choose an ATS and I set up some forms on Tally so we didn't miss out on candidates in the interim. What a breeze! I duplicated a form from the templates. Loved the process, super easy. I used it for some other personal use cases as well, it was always smooth. Integrates perfectly with Notion.
dislikes: I don't have anything bad to say, it's great.
problems_solved: Application forms for hiring ahead of having a tool set up

likes: The conditional logic works! My form has so many of them-- hiding and showing blocks, jumping to pages-- but tested a few times and the logic works!
dislikes: Since everyone starts with a blank page, it takes a while to get the mechanics of laying out blocks.
problems_solved: The form I built with Tally is my client consult form. It used to require me a lot of time asking clients their issues, then them typing out long answers. My Tally form helps shorten that interview, frees up my time, my clients' times too.

likes: I love the flexibility to create various types of forms that I wasn't able to create using Google forms or Microsoft forms.
dislikes: Although the desktop interface works well, the matrix form I created doesn't fit well on the screen when users access it from a mobile device.
problems_solved: Attendees to my course are now able to register for multiple modules over multiple dates using a simple user friendly form

likes: Tally was super easy to set up and start using, and the user interface is awesome.
dislikes: I didn't find any red flags. everything is smooth.
problems_solved: I always wanted an elegant form builder to engage with my audience, and tally has been the perfect choice.

reviewer.name: Verified User in Computer & Network Security

likes: It took a minute to figure out the logic, etc., but once you can see how it works, it is simple to use. The customer support stood by to ensure I got the result I wanted which was the key to my success. Many thanks.
dislikes: It took a little trial and error to figure out without help
problems_solved: It's helping determine the correct product offering for my audience.

reviewer.name: Amber Y.
reviewer.role: Owner, Project Manager, Designer, Sales & Marketing Director, Customer Service Manager, Human Resources Director, Office Manager

likes: The ease of use on Tally is incomparable.
dislikes: There really is nothing to dislike about Tally. It is easy to use, and it walks you through the steps; when combined with other AI, it can be an incredibly helpful tool.
problems_solved: It is converting followers into email and name lead generations.

likes: The clean interface and the way I was able to match it to my brand! And how you can probably make it do anything you need it to.
dislikes: There's a learning curve and it can be a bit hard to figure out exactly what you need to set up in the beginning.
problems_solved: I was able to create my own custom scorecard-style quiz for free. This allows me to provide value to my audience and grow my email list.

likes: The epitome of a product-led company; unmatched functionality, beautiful Notion-like UX, enterprise-grade integrations, and incredibly generous free tier.
dislikes: None. Zero. Absolutely incredible tool. Well I guess recurring payment functionality would be good.
problems_solved: Data integration across Notion and Sheets with API-ready connectivity as we scale.

likes: I love the flexibility. I'm able to do things with Tally, such as embed videos and have a calendar booking on the base tier. These are expensive upgrades in other tools like Typeform.
dislikes: Maybe just that it doesn't have a visual representation of the logic flows. But that's mostly because my prior experience with Typeform kinda set that expectation.
problems_solved: Im using Tally to create a lead generation form for free and its allowing me the customizations I want to help strengthen conversions.

likes: Its ease of use and customization options for the forms makes it a wonderful product. It integrates with various other tools, which is helpful for workflow automation. All in all it is a wonderful product.
dislikes: Nothing as such but few more customization options could be real handy
problems_solved: It addresses the common challenges of form creation by providing an intuitive, customizable and cost effective platform. It is not complex for form building.

likes: The customer service team was amazing,they truly saved my day! I really appreciated their help and support.
dislikes: I wish there were more features available in the freemium model. It would make the experience even better. Haha.
problems_solved: All in one place

likes: It is a simple, easy-to-use, but very powerful form builder. I have my lead generation forms when people arrive at my website, my sign-up forms for when people want a quote for my services, my forms to collect sign-ups for a webinar, and all forms that I use at my company have been made with tally- This has been working for me for over 9 months. I intend to continue using their services as we scale. :)
dislikes: Not much really- perhaps if i had to really pick out one, I would say that the selection tool inside the form builder could be further optimized. This has been feeling a little choppy at present. But believe me, this is really not that big of a deal for the ever improving software they offer. Kudos to the team as they are super responsive in addressing any and all problems..
problems_solved: Streamline data collection and lead generation for me and my team. Plus very easy to integrate into our shopify website.

likes: The customization and automation of the smart form
dislikes:
The organization of pages when the form is long. It would be interesting to have the option to choose the page directly without scrolling through the pages one by one with the mouse.

The form bugs a lot when there are many pages; the appearance of the text is very slow.
problems_solved: Automated information collection

likes:
Tally has been a game-changer for my business. As someone who deals with a lot of forms, data collection, and client-facing tasks, Tally makes everything so much simpler. The interface is clean, intuitive, and user-friendly, which means no steep learning curve. I especially love how customizable the forms are, from branding to logic jumps.

The best part? It integrates seamlessly with other tools I use, making the entire workflow smoother. It’s not just a form builder – it’s an essential part of my process for gathering information, automating data flows, and keeping everything organized. If you're looking for an easy-to-use, yet powerful tool to manage forms and surveys, Tally is a must-try. Highly recommended!
dislikes: Can't really think of anything, maybe i wish there was a white label format of it?
problems_solved: We use it for lead magnets and lead captures

likes:
You can create any forms in a Notion-like editor. It offers everything you need by just typing "/" and then choosing the needed actions.

When you publish your form, it does not limit the number of form view rates. You can create unlimited forms for free. Also, the UX is so freaking easy and amazing. They have redefined the boring form creation process.

We're using Tally Forms to get feedback, collect data, activate redemption codes, and manage our support tickets.
dislikes: I do not have anything to say about the negative sides of Tally. They are a community-driven company, so they listen to their clients through an honest feature request pool.
problems_solved: We handle our product's support tickets with Tally's embeddable form. We activate redemption codes with Tally's webhooks, we collect feedback with Tally. They provide anything we need.

likes: simplicity, speed, and comprehensive business management capabilities
dislikes: It iss not a "cloud-native" application.
problems_solved: its very good with accounting

likes: Tally is easy to use as an administrator and end user. Also Tally offers excellent value for money unlike most of its competitors. Support is also very responsive and helpful. Id highly recommend Tally.
dislikes: I have no dislikes at all. I wish Tally great success in the market.
problems_solved: I often need to create custom forms, and Tally ticks all my boxes

likes: I loved that Tally is fully free e customizable. I also liked the fact that it is super easy to use amd tk connect to other software
dislikes: I didn't found relevant element that i disliked avout Tally
problems_solved: Finding a completely free sourvey app that enables you to have unlimited spurveys and subscriptions

likes: 95% of functionality I need, all for free.
dislikes: Scrolling through previews takes too long. Would be nice to be able to just open a single page straight in preview, rather than having to click the whole form.
problems_solved: Creating forms & surveys for events and clients.

likes: Very much like notion, lots of options but not in your face. Very intuitive and great options to have in my forms.
dislikes: Does not integrate great with MS but I think that's MS's fault.
problems_solved: Events - filling out forms and getting better data from it.

likes: The UI/UX is perfect. Nothing more than what is required. I also love the conditional logic functionality. Straight forward and easy to use. Customer service is also very helpful.
dislikes: Nothing major - I cannot share single forms with colleagues - I'd have to give them access to the whole workspace with sensitive info that I don't want to share.
problems_solved: I need a form builder - found Tally - does what I need!

likes: The design is modern, clear, and can be wonderfully integrated into one's own brand presence. Initially, I had a problem because no notification emails were reaching me. The support team quickly and personally took care of my issue – and lovingly solved the problem. Since then, everything has been running smoothly. Absolute recommendation!
dislikes: I haven't noticed any. After the support, everything is running great.
problems_solved: Initially, the problem was that the self mail notification did not arrive.

likes: It's very easy to use with so a lot of functionality for a very reasonable price. I switched from Typeform because of how expensive it was, and I didn't like the user experience of creating forms as much as I do with Tally.
dislikes: Limits on what kind of documents I can receive. I need to collect passport photos for a form and it's against their policy to do so.
problems_solved: Collecting information in a nice and simple way that I can easily review as data.

reviewer.name: Verified User in Professional Training & Coaching

likes: Flexible, easy and fairly priced. Actively developed and good support.
dislikes: For some reason, a single question typically includes many blocks that are not visually linked in the editor. Not a big deal, but good to know when you're dragging stuff around.
problems_solved: Easy form creation, even on mobile.

likes: I like the simplicity of it but it's still so useful and powerful. And it honestly kinda fun setting up forms in it

dislikes: I haven't seen any disadvantages so far, I think in the future it could have integration with more types of tools. I don't use the paid Tally but if one day I need it, I will definitely consider subscribing.

likes: so easy to use really great with embed,......

likes: Its Simple , reliable, and pricing is transparent and fair

reviewer.name: Roberto D.
reviewer.role: Singer, performer and vocal tecnique and improvisation teacher.

likes: I've been utilizing Count structures for over 1 year now, this is the best structure administration i've each utilized. I've purchased count master and i'm extremely content with it. Best thing about I like in count master is halfway submisions. What's more, the webhook combination works semeasly, I utilize this as my everyday structure and i'm extremely content with their administration.
dislikes: The negative parts of Count incorporate that a few mixes just work through Zapier, and modified space names are just accessible in the paid form. Nonetheless, these disadvantages don't decrease the various positive parts of the product.
problems_solved: Count is tackling the interest for structures and reactions by offering a free variant, dissimilar as far as possible the quantity of reactions or devises in their free game plans. This permits organizations to zero in on estimating client criticism without agonizing over limits.

likes: All the features it has in a freemium plan is amazing!! So happy with Tally!!
dislikes: Nothing to comment that dislikes me. It's free, so for what I get is a 10 out of 10!
problems_solved: It's our go-to-place to create our Applications form, Test submissions form, and leads generation forms

likes:
- Supereasy to use, but also very powerful.

- Integrations.

- Free tier more than enough.

Tally is much more advanced: you can build a no-code solution (for budgets, calculators, quizzes...) or just a fast form to collect responses.

It is ver very good.
dislikes: It could have more personalization, but for me is ok.
problems_solved: With tally you can build nice forms, but you can also create more complex solutions that involves even payments

likes: I've been using Tally forms for more than 1 year now, this is the best form service i've every used. I've bought tally pro and i'm very happy with it. Best thing about I like in tally pro is partial submisions. And the webhook integration works semeasly, I use this as my daily form and i'm very happy with their service.
dislikes: The pro plan is a bit expencive, other than that there is nothing I dislike about tally forms.
problems_solved: Partial submissions, seamless integration with Google sheets, Notion, webhook, zapier, google analytics.

likes: The Tally.so software is a web application that allows for the free creation of unlimited forms. One of the standout positive features is its simplicity, as it requires no coding skills. Additionally, the free plan offers unlimited forms and responses, the ability to customize the thank you page, and free integrations, making it easy to incorporate into the company website. The documentation is comprehensive, and the form can be published in multiple languages, making it an excellent option for a variety of businesses.
dislikes: The negative aspects of Tally include that some integrations only work through Zapier, and customized domain names are only available in the paid version. However, these drawbacks do not diminish the numerous positive aspects of the software.
problems_solved: Tally is solving the demand for forms and responses by offering a free version, unlike its competitors who limit the number of responses or forms in their free plans. This allows businesses to focus on measuring customer feedback without worrying about limitations.

likes: they release new features constantly, and free tier is very generous.
dislikes: Actualy Nothing from product. Only thing I miss is a direct One Drive Integration.
problems_solved: Google Forms are uggly, microsoft one doesnt have enough features like prefilled fields.

likes: Form integration, especially Notion integration.
dislikes: When I create a form, it takes me a long time.
problems_solved: The way of integrating with Notion.

likes: Tally is the ultimate solution for businesses, it streamlines not only accounting but also stock management, payroll and other tasks. It is truly superior to other software options.
dislikes: While overall the software is functional, it can experience slow performance and unexpected closures. If these technical issues could be resolved, it would greatly benefit the user experience. Aside from these issues, the software is satisfactory.
problems_solved: This software fully meets our needs in terms of accounting, inventory management, and payroll activities. It has greatly increased productivity for me, and it allows us to easily track all aspects of our business.

likes: I love the fact that Tally breaks down conventional form building mechanism. The text-based approach to form-building allows for a great user experience all around.
dislikes: I don't like how confusing conditional formatting is on Tally. Besides that, I also believe that Tally requires too much clickthrough and that's painful to go through.
problems_solved: I'm a student, and tally is letting me build a lot of forms for surveys that I have to conduct regularly. In my workplace, I also utilize tally to collect user feedback & infromation.

likes: Really Tally is One Stop solution for Businesses. It helps not only your accouning work as well as Stock Managemnt and Payroll Activities and other work. It's definitely better than other sofetware
dislikes: Everything is fine but some time it gets slow more and sometimes it closed automatically. If Technical team can resolve it. It will be better for the user. Rest is fine.
problems_solved: It solveing all our need as like Accounting, Inventory Management, Payroll activities etc. Yes it is more productive for me by this we can easily track everything of our business.

likes: Tally is a perfect application for accounting management, in our software development company it has helped a lot in simplifying payments and quotes each month, in addition to being easy to use, it has a wide variety of templates and innovative designs.
dislikes: the tally itself is a great app, but it has a lot of competition in the market, which doesn't make it stand out that much. Its free use keeps its functions basic and simple, allowing easy access but limiting its ability to expand in the market.
recommendations: I recommend tally 100% for those small to medium caliber companies. It makes accounting work easy and simple, but this app is limited to tackle bigger challenges and accounts when it comes to big industries.
problems_solved: my work we develop software for a high range of clients, so the development of this software is very varied, as well as its cost. tally helps us to summarize the accounting books with its basic and easy-to-use functions, it is in charge of supporting us with tools that simplify the company's accounting day by day.

likes: The simplicity of the UI helps you to see the summary view, detailed submission view and integration tab to visualize or automate further using options like Airtable. The presentation of the data is great, it helps you to analyze it very quickly.
dislikes: To be honest I am a fan of Tally after using it and I don't have a list of features I dislike particularly. As an Indian user I am a bit price sensitive and hence if the Tallypro subscription price can be reduced keeping the Indian market in the mind then nothing like that, rest I love this product.
problems_solved: I am trying to collect the data from my landing page to gauge the interest of the audience in my product. The benefits are the organization of data in one place, connecting the data to various other platforms like Notion, Airtable to automate some of the workflows.

likes:
The simplicity of the platform, it's so sleek and lightweight that it makes the loading super responsive!

Almost 90% of their features are available in their free plan that even makes their Premium offering hard to sell!
dislikes:
We often feel that some essential functions aren't available on the platform, making the job challenging!

They should let the users customize the themes according to their branding schemes.
problems_solved: We want to collect responses for the online classes on various use cases! For the faculties to collect responses, the form creation process is super effortless.

likes: You can easily create and customize your own forms, or choose from the many templated forms available such as a registration form, online quiz, job application, contact form, etc.
dislikes: Although there are options as far as customizing the cover photo, logo and page, there aren't as many color and font options as I would like to see. Still works though!
problems_solved: I am creating fillable forms that I can share with my team to get feedback, or share with clients to get intake information, and I can save time by using a template that's already created, or if I have the time, create my own.

likes: The builder is intuitive, and sharing access with clients and team members for collaboration is simple.
dislikes: You can't hide the title or change much on the customer-facing UI.
recommendations: Tally is an excellent alternative to Typeform with one straightforward plan for all of the features.
problems_solved: I am making onboarding experiences integrated with customer databases and web apps.

likes: Tally is one of the most commonly used accounting software. It is easy to learn and implement in a small size of organization with effective output results. The investment is small for SME
dislikes:
Despite of having so many good features that Tally offer but unfortunately you feel limited when the organization grows, complexity of the accounts increases.

It provides limited reporting compare to other competitors
problems_solved:
Inventory management

Basic account management

Legder and balance sheets

Easy to learn and implement

Cost effective and reliable tool for new startups and sme to automate their accouting

dislikes: The template gallery could be better and include more templates. I believe it will be improved over time.
problems_solved: We are using it on our customer-facing forms for both lead generation and customer support requests.
